163	@pass_context
164	def _gettext_alias(
165	    __context: Context, *args: t.Any, **kwargs: t.Any
166	) -> t.Union[t.Any, Undefined]:
167	    return __context.call(__context.resolve("gettext"), *args, **kwargs)
168	
169	
170	def _make_new_gettext(func: t.Callable[[str], str]) -> t.Callable[..., str]:
171	    @pass_context
172	    def gettext(__context: Context, __string: str, **variables: t.Any) -> str:
173	        rv = __context.call(func, __string)
174	        if __context.eval_ctx.autoescape:
175	            rv = Markup(rv)
176	        # Always treat as a format string, even if there are no
177	        # variables. This makes translation strings more consistent
178	        # and predictable. This requires escaping
179	        return rv % variables  # type: ignore
180	
181	    return gettext
182	
183	
184	def _make_new_ngettext(func: t.Callable[[str, str, int], str]) -> t.Callable[..., str]:
185	    @pass_context
186	    def ngettext(
187	        __context: Context,
188	        __singular: str,
189	        __plural: str,
190	        __num: int,
191	        **variables: t.Any,
192	    ) -> str:
193	        variables.setdefault("num", __num)
194	        rv = __context.call(func, __singular, __plural, __num)
195	        if __context.eval_ctx.autoescape:
196	            rv = Markup(rv)
197	        # Always treat as a format string, see gettext comment above.
198	        return rv % variables  # type: ignore
199	
200	    return ngettext
201	
202	
203	def _make_new_dgettext(func: t.Callable[[str, str], str]) -> t.Callable[..., str]:
204	    @pass_context
205	    def dgettext(
206	        __context: Context, __domain: str, __string: str, **variables: t.Any
207	    ) -> str:
208	        rv = __context.call(func, __domain, __string)
209	        if __context.eval_ctx.autoescape:
210	            rv = Markup(rv)
211	        # Always treat as a format string, even if there are no
212	        # variables. This makes translation strings more consistent
213	        # and predictable. This requires escaping
214	        return rv % variables  # type: ignore
215	
216	    return dgettext
217	
218	
219	def _make_new_dngettext(
220	    func: t.Callable[[str, str, str, int], str]
221	) -> t.Callable[..., str]:
222	    @pass_context
223	    def dngettext(
224	        __context: Context,
225	        __domain: str,
226	        __singular: str,
227	        __plural: str,
228	        __num: int,
229	        **variables: t.Any,
230	    ) -> str:
231	        variables.setdefault("num", __num)
232	        rv = __context.call(func, __domain, __singular, __plural, __num)
233	        if __context.eval_ctx.autoescape:
234	            rv = Markup(rv)
235	        # Always treat as a format string, see gettext comment above.
236	        return rv % variables  # type: ignore
237	
238	    return dngettext
239	
240	
241	def _make_new_pgettext(func: t.Callable[[str, str], str]) -> t.Callable[..., str]:
242	    @pass_context
243	    def pgettext(
244	        __context: Context, __string_ctx: str, __string: str, **variables: t.Any
245	    ) -> str:
246	        variables.setdefault("context", __string_ctx)
247	        rv = __context.call(func, __string_ctx, __string)
248	
249	        if __context.eval_ctx.autoescape:
250	            rv = Markup(rv)
251	
252	        # Always treat as a format string, see gettext comment above.
253	        return rv % variables  # type: ignore
254	
255	    return pgettext
256	
257	
258	def _make_new_npgettext(
259	    func: t.Callable[[str, str, str, int], str]
260	) -> t.Callable[..., str]:
261	    @pass_context
262	    def npgettext(
263	        __context: Context,
264	        __string_ctx: str,
265	        __singular: str,
266	        __plural: str,
267	        __num: int,
268	        **variables: t.Any,
269	    ) -> str:
270	        variables.setdefault("context", __string_ctx)
271	        variables.setdefault("num", __num)
272	        rv = __context.call(func, __string_ctx, __singular, __plural, __num)
273	
274	        if __context.eval_ctx.autoescape:
275	            rv = Markup(rv)
276	
277	        # Always treat as a format string, see gettext comment above.
278	        return rv % variables  # type: ignore
279	
280	    return npgettext

283	class InternationalizationExtension(Extension):
284	    """This extension adds gettext support to Jinja."""
285	
286	    tags = {"trans"}
287	
288	    # TODO: the i18n extension is currently reevaluating values in a few
289	    # situations.  Take this example:
290	    #   {% trans count=something() %}{{ count }} foo{% pluralize
291	    #     %}{{ count }} fooss{% endtrans %}
292	    # something is called twice here.  One time for the gettext value and
293	    # the other time for the n-parameter of the ngettext function.
294	
295	    def __init__(self, environment: Environment) -> None:
296	        super().__init__(environment)
297	        environment.globals["_"] = _gettext_alias
298	        environment.extend(
299	            install_gettext_translations=self._install,
300	            install_null_translations=self._install_null,
301	            install_gettext_callables=self._install_callables,
302	            uninstall_gettext_translations=self._uninstall,
303	            extract_translations=self._extract,
304	            newstyle_gettext=False,
305	        )
306	
307	    def _install(
308	        self, translations: "_SupportedTranslations", newstyle: t.Optional[bool] = None
309	    ) -> None:
310	        # ugettext and ungettext are preferred in case the I18N library
311	        # is providing compatibility with older Python versions.
312	        gettext = getattr(translations, "ugettext", None)
313	        if gettext is None:
314	            gettext = translations.gettext
315	        ngettext = getattr(translations, "ungettext", None)
316	        if ngettext is None:
317	            ngettext = translations.ngettext
318	
319	        pgettext = getattr(translations, "pgettext", None)
320	        npgettext = getattr(translations, "npgettext", None)
321	        dgettext = getattr(translations, "dgettext", None)
322	        dngettext = getattr(translations, "dngettext", None)
323	        self._install_callables(
324	            gettext,
325	            ngettext,
326	            newstyle=newstyle,
327	            pgettext=pgettext,
328	            npgettext=npgettext,
329	            dgettext=dgettext,
330	            dngettext=dngettext,
331	        )
332	
333	    def _install_null(self, newstyle: t.Optional[bool] = None) -> None:
334	        import gettext
335	
336	        translations = gettext.NullTranslations()
337	
338	        if hasattr(translations, "pgettext"):
339	            # Python < 3.8
340	            pgettext = translations.pgettext  # type: ignore
341	        else:
342	
343	            def pgettext(c: str, s: str) -> str:
344	                return s
345	
346	        if hasattr(translations, "npgettext"):
347	            npgettext = translations.npgettext  # type: ignore
348	        else:
349	
350	            def npgettext(c: str, s: str, p: str, n: int) -> str:
351	                return s if n == 1 else p
352	
353	        if hasattr(translations, "dgettext"):
354	            dgettext = translations.dgettext  # type: ignore
355	        else:
356	
357	            def dgettext(d: str, s: str) -> str:
358	                return translations.gettext(s)
359	
360	        if hasattr(translations, "dngettext"):
361	            dngettext = translations.dngettext  # type: ignore
362	        else:
363	
364	            def dngettext(d: str, s: str, p: str, n: int) -> str:
365	                return translations.ngettext(s, p, n)
366	
367	        self._install_callables(
368	            gettext=translations.gettext,
369	            ngettext=translations.ngettext,
370	            newstyle=newstyle,
371	            pgettext=pgettext,
372	            npgettext=npgettext,
373	            dgettext=dgettext,
374	            dngettext=dngettext,
375	        )
376	
377	    def _install_callables(
378	        self,
379	        gettext: t.Callable[[str], str],
380	        ngettext: t.Callable[[str, str, int], str],
381	        newstyle: t.Optional[bool] = None,
382	        pgettext: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str, str], str]] = None,
383	        npgettext: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str, str, str, int], str]] = None,
384	        dgettext: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str, str], str]] = None,
385	        dngettext: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str, str, str, int], str]] = None,
386	    ) -> None:
387	        if newstyle is not None:
388	            self.environment.newstyle_gettext = newstyle  # type: ignore
389	        if self.environment.newstyle_gettext:  # type: ignore
390	            gettext = _make_new_gettext(gettext)
391	            ngettext = _make_new_ngettext(ngettext)
392	
393	            if pgettext is not None:
394	                pgettext = _make_new_pgettext(pgettext)
395	
396	            if npgettext is not None:
397	                npgettext = _make_new_npgettext(npgettext)
398	
399	            if dgettext is not None:
400	                dgettext = _make_new_dgettext(dgettext)
401	
402	            if dngettext is not None:
403	                dngettext = _make_new_dngettext(dngettext)
404	
405	        self.environment.globals.update(
406	            gettext=gettext,
407	            ngettext=ngettext,
408	            pgettext=pgettext,
409	            npgettext=npgettext,
410	            dgettext=dgettext,
411	            dngettext=dngettext,
412	        )
413	
414	    def _uninstall(self, translations: "_SupportedTranslations") -> None:
415	        for key in (
416	            "gettext",
417	            "ngettext",
418	            "pgettext",
419	            "npgettext",
420	            "dgettext",
421	            "dngettext",
422	        ):
423	            self.environment.globals.pop(key, None)
424	
425	    def _extract(
426	        self,
427	        source: t.Union[str, nodes.Template],
428	        gettext_functions: t.Sequence[str] = GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS,
429	    ) -> t.Iterator[
430	        t.Tuple[int, str, t.Union[t.Optional[str], t.Tuple[t.Optional[str], ...]]]
431	    ]:
432	        if isinstance(source, str):
433	            source = self.environment.parse(source)
434	        return extract_from_ast(source, gettext_functions)
435	
436	    def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
437	        """Parse a translatable tag."""
438	        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
439	        num_called_num = False
440	
441	        # find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
442	        # defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at
443	        # a later state.
444	        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
445	        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
446	        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
447	        trimmed = None
448	        domain: t.Optional[str] = None
449	        while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
450	            if variables:
451	                parser.stream.expect("comma")
452	
453	            # skip colon for python compatibility
454	            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
455	                break
456	
457	            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
458	            if token.value in variables:
459	                parser.fail(
460	                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
461	                    token.lineno,
462	                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
463	                )
464	
465	            # expressions
466	            if token.value == "domain" and parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
467	                next(parser.stream)
468	                if domain is not None:
469	                    parser.fail(
470	                        "translation domain given twice.",
471	                        token.lineno,
472	                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
473	                    )
474	                domain_lineno = parser.stream.current.lineno
475	                domain_expr = parser.parse_expression()
476	                if not isinstance(domain_expr, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(
477	                    domain_expr.value, str
478	                ):
479	                    parser.fail(
480	                        "translation domain must be a string literal (constant),"
481	                        " not a variable.",
482	                        domain_lineno,
483	                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
484	                    )
485	                domain = domain_expr.value
486	                # allow a following comma even though "domain" isn't
487	                # tracked in `variables`, so it can be freely mixed in
488	                # with the other comma-separated parameters
489	                parser.stream.skip_if("comma")
490	                continue
491	            elif parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
492	                next(parser.stream)
493	                variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
494	            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
495	                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
496	                continue
497	            else:
498	                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")
499	
500	            if plural_expr is None:
501	                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
502	                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
503	                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
504	                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
505	                        nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var
506	                    )
507	                else:
508	                    plural_expr = var
509	                num_called_num = token.value == "num"
510	
511	        parser.stream.expect("block_end")
512	
513	        plural = None
514	        have_plural = False
515	        referenced = set()
516	
517	        # now parse until endtrans or pluralize
518	        singular_names, singular = self._parse_block(parser, True)
519	        if singular_names:
520	            referenced.update(singular_names)
521	            if plural_expr is None:
522	                plural_expr = nodes.Name(singular_names[0], "load")
523	                num_called_num = singular_names[0] == "num"
524	
525	        # if we have a pluralize block, we parse that too
526	        if parser.stream.current.test("name:pluralize"):
527	            have_plural = True
528	            next(parser.stream)
529	            if parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
530	                token = parser.stream.expect("name")
531	                if token.value not in variables:
532	                    parser.fail(
533	                        f"unknown variable {token.value!r} for pluralization",
534	                        token.lineno,
535	                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
536	                    )
537	                plural_expr = variables[token.value]
538	                num_called_num = token.value == "num"
539	            parser.stream.expect("block_end")
540	            plural_names, plural = self._parse_block(parser, False)
541	            next(parser.stream)
542	            referenced.update(plural_names)
543	        else:
544	            next(parser.stream)
545	
546	        # register free names as simple name expressions
547	        for name in referenced:
548	            if name not in variables:
549	                variables[name] = nodes.Name(name, "load")
550	
551	        if not have_plural:
552	            plural_expr = None
553	        elif plural_expr is None:
554	            parser.fail("pluralize without variables", lineno)
555	
556	        if trimmed is None:
557	            trimmed = self.environment.policies["ext.i18n.trimmed"]
558	        if trimmed:
559	            singular = self._trim_whitespace(singular)
560	            if plural:
561	                plural = self._trim_whitespace(plural)
562	
563	        node = self._make_node(
564	            singular,
565	            plural,
566	            variables,
567	            plural_expr,
568	            bool(referenced),
569	            num_called_num and have_plural,
570	            domain,
571	        )
572	        node.set_lineno(lineno)
573	        if plural_expr_assignment is not None:
574	            return [plural_expr_assignment, node]
575	        else:
576	            return node
577	
578	    def _trim_whitespace(self, string: str, _ws_re: t.Pattern[str] = _ws_re) -> str:
579	        return _ws_re.sub(" ", string.strip())
580	
581	    def _parse_block(
582	        self, parser: "Parser", allow_pluralize: bool
583	    ) -> t.Tuple[t.List[str], str]:
584	        """Parse until the next block tag with a given name."""
585	        referenced = []
586	        buf = []
587	
588	        while True:
589	            if parser.stream.current.type == "data":
590	                buf.append(parser.stream.current.value.replace("%", "%%"))
591	                next(parser.stream)
592	            elif parser.stream.current.type == "variable_begin":
593	                next(parser.stream)
594	                name = parser.stream.expect("name").value
595	                referenced.append(name)
596	                buf.append(f"%({name})s")
597	                parser.stream.expect("variable_end")
598	            elif parser.stream.current.type == "block_begin":
599	                next(parser.stream)
600	                if parser.stream.current.test("name:endtrans"):
601	                    break
602	                elif parser.stream.current.test("name:pluralize"):
603	                    if allow_pluralize:
604	                        break
605	                    parser.fail(
606	                        "a translatable section can have only one pluralize section"
607	                    )
608	                parser.fail(
609	                    "control structures in translatable sections are not allowed"
610	                )
611	            elif parser.stream.eos:
612	                parser.fail("unclosed translation block")
613	            else:
614	                raise RuntimeError("internal parser error")
615	
616	        return referenced, concat(buf)
617	
618	    def _make_node(
619	        self,
620	        singular: str,
621	        plural: t.Optional[str],
622	        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
623	        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
624	        vars_referenced: bool,
625	        num_called_num: bool,
626	        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
627	    ) -> nodes.Output:
628	        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
629	        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
630	        node: nodes.Expr
631	
632	        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
633	        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
634	        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
635	            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
636	            if plural:
637	                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")
638	
639	        # singular only:
640	        if plural_expr is None:
641	            if domain is not None:
642	                func_name = "dgettext"
643	                func_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = [
644	                    nodes.Const(domain),
645	                    nodes.Const(singular),
646	                ]
647	            else:
648	                func_name = "gettext"
649	                func_args = [nodes.Const(singular)]
650	            node = nodes.Call(nodes.Name(func_name, "load"), func_args, [], None, None)
651	
652	        # singular and plural
653	        else:
654	            if domain is not None:
655	                func_name = "dngettext"
656	                func_args = [
657	                    nodes.Const(domain),
658	                    nodes.Const(singular),
659	                    nodes.Const(plural),
660	                    plural_expr,
661	                ]
662	            else:
663	                func_name = "ngettext"
664	                func_args = [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr]
665	            node = nodes.Call(nodes.Name(func_name, "load"), func_args, [], None, None)
666	
667	        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
668	        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
669	        # handling itself
670	        if newstyle:
671	            for key, value in variables.items():
672	                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
673	                # called num, so just skip it.
674	                if num_called_num and key == "num":
675	                    continue
676	                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))
677	
678	        # otherwise do that here
679	        else:
680	            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
681	            # environment with autoescaping turned on
682	            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
683	            if variables:
684	                node = nodes.Mod(
685	                    node,
686	                    nodes.Dict(
687	                        [
688	                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
689	                            for key, value in variables.items()
690	                        ]
691	                    ),
692	                )
693	        return nodes.Output([node])
694	
695	
696	class ExprStmtExtension(Extension):
697	    """Adds a `do` tag to Jinja that works like the print statement just
698	    that it doesn't print the return value.
699	    """
700	
701	    tags = {"do"}
702	
703	    def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> nodes.ExprStmt:
704	        node = nodes.ExprStmt(lineno=next(parser.stream).lineno)
705	        node.node = parser.parse_tuple()
706	        return node
707	
708	
709	class LoopControlExtension(Extension):
710	    """Adds break and continue to the template engine."""
711	
712	    tags = {"break", "continue"}
713	
714	    def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Break, nodes.Continue]:
715	        token = next(parser.stream)
716	        if token.value == "break":
717	            return nodes.Break(lineno=token.lineno)
718	        return nodes.Continue(lineno=token.lineno)
719	
720	
721	class DebugExtension(Extension):
722	    """A ``{% debug %}`` tag that dumps the available variables,
723	    filters, and tests.
724	
725	    .. code-block:: html+jinja
726	
727	        <pre>{% debug %}</pre>
728	
729	    .. code-block:: text
730	
731	        {'context': {'cycler': <class 'jinja2.utils.Cycler'>,
732	                     ...,
733	                     'namespace': <class 'jinja2.utils.Namespace'>},
734	         'filters': ['abs', 'attr', 'batch', 'capitalize', 'center', 'count', 'd',
735	                     ..., 'urlencode', 'urlize', 'wordcount', 'wordwrap', 'xmlattr'],
736	         'tests': ['!=', '<', '<=', '==', '>', '>=', 'callable', 'defined',
737	                   ..., 'odd', 'sameas', 'sequence', 'string', 'undefined', 'upper']}
738	
739	    .. versionadded:: 2.11.0
740	    """
741	
742	    tags = {"debug"}
743	
744	    def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> nodes.Output:
745	        lineno = parser.stream.expect("name:debug").lineno
746	        context = nodes.ContextReference()
747	        result = self.call_method("_render", [context], lineno=lineno)
748	        return nodes.Output([result], lineno=lineno)
749	
750	    def _render(self, context: Context) -> str:
751	        result = {
752	            "context": context.get_all(),
753	            "filters": sorted(self.environment.filters.keys()),
754	            "tests": sorted(self.environment.tests.keys()),
755	        }
756	
757	        # Set the depth since the intent is to show the top few names.
758	        return pprint.pformat(result, depth=3, compact=True)
759	
760	
761	def extract_from_ast(
762	    ast: nodes.Template,
763	    gettext_functions: t.Sequence[str] = GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS,
764	    babel_style: bool = True,
765	) -> t.Iterator[
766	    t.Tuple[int, str, t.Union[t.Optional[str], t.Tuple[t.Optional[str], ...]]]
767	]:
768	    """Extract localizable strings from the given template node.  Per
769	    default this function returns matches in babel style that means non string
770	    parameters as well as keyword arguments are returned as `None`.  This
771	    allows Babel to figure out what you really meant if you are using
772	    gettext functions that allow keyword arguments for placeholder expansion.
773	    If you don't want that behavior set the `babel_style` parameter to `False`
774	    which causes only strings to be returned and parameters are always stored
775	    in tuples.  As a consequence invalid gettext calls (calls without a single
776	    string parameter or string parameters after non-string parameters) are
777	    skipped.
778	
779	    This example explains the behavior:
780	
781	    >>> from jinja2 import Environment
782	    >>> env = Environment()
783	    >>> node = env.parse('{{ (_("foo"), _(), ngettext("foo", "bar", 42)) }}')
784	    >>> list(extract_from_ast(node))
785	    [(1, '_', 'foo'), (1, '_', ()), (1, 'ngettext', ('foo', 'bar', None))]
786	    >>> list(extract_from_ast(node, babel_style=False))
787	    [(1, '_', ('foo',)), (1, 'ngettext', ('foo', 'bar'))]
788	
789	    For every string found this function yields a ``(lineno, function,
790	    message)`` tuple, where:
791	
792	    * ``lineno`` is the number of the line on which the string was found,
793	    * ``function`` is the name of the ``gettext`` function used (if the
794	      string was extracted from embedded Python code), and
795	    *   ``message`` is the string, or a tuple of strings for functions
796	         with multiple string arguments.
797	
798	    This extraction function operates on the AST and is because of that unable
799	    to extract any comments.  For comment support you have to use the babel
800	    extraction interface or extract comments yourself.
801	    """
802	    out: t.Union[t.Optional[str], t.Tuple[t.Optional[str], ...]]
803	
804	    for node in ast.find_all(nodes.Call):
805	        if (
806	            not isinstance(node.node, nodes.Name)
807	            or node.node.name not in gettext_functions
808	        ):
809	            continue
810	
811	        strings: t.List[t.Optional[str]] = []
812	
813	        for arg in node.args:
814	            if isinstance(arg, nodes.Const) and isinstance(arg.value, str):
815	                strings.append(arg.value)
816	            else:
817	                strings.append(None)
818	
819	        for _ in node.kwargs:
820	            strings.append(None)
821	        if node.dyn_args is not None:
822	            strings.append(None)
823	        if node.dyn_kwargs is not None:
824	            strings.append(None)
825	
826	        if not babel_style:
827	            out = tuple(x for x in strings if x is not None)
828	
829	            if not out:
830	                continue
831	        else:
832	            if len(strings) == 1:
833	                out = strings[0]
834	            else:
835	                out = tuple(strings)
836	
837	        yield node.lineno, node.node.name, out
838	
839	
840	class _CommentFinder:
841	    """Helper class to find comments in a token stream.  Can only
842	    find comments for gettext calls forwards.  Once the comment
843	    from line 4 is found, a comment for line 1 will not return a
844	    usable value.
845	    """
846	
847	    def __init__(
848	        self, tokens: t.Sequence[t.Tuple[int, str, str]], comment_tags: t.Sequence[str]
849	    ) -> None:
850	        self.tokens = tokens
851	        self.comment_tags = comment_tags
852	        self.offset = 0
853	        self.last_lineno = 0
854	
855	    def find_backwards(self, offset: int) -> t.List[str]:
856	        try:
857	            for _, token_type, token_value in reversed(
858	                self.tokens[self.offset : offset]
859	            ):
860	                if token_type in ("comment", "linecomment"):
861	                    try:
862	                        prefix, comment = token_value.split(None, 1)
863	                    except ValueError:
864	                        continue
865	                    if prefix in self.comment_tags:
866	                        return [comment.rstrip()]
867	            return []
868	        finally:
869	            self.offset = offset
870	
871	    def find_comments(self, lineno: int) -> t.List[str]:
872	        if not self.comment_tags or self.last_lineno > lineno:
873	            return []
874	        for idx, (token_lineno, _, _) in enumerate(self.tokens[self.offset :]):
875	            if token_lineno > lineno:
876	                return self.find_backwards(self.offset + idx)
877	        return self.find_backwards(len(self.tokens))
878	
879	
880	def babel_extract(
881	    fileobj: t.BinaryIO,
882	    keywords: t.Sequence[str],
883	    comment_tags: t.Sequence[str],
884	    options: t.Dict[str, t.Any],
885	) -> t.Iterator[
886	    t.Tuple[
887	        int, str, t.Union[t.Optional[str], t.Tuple[t.Optional[str], ...]], t.List[str]
888	    ]
889	]:
890	    """Babel extraction method for Jinja templates.
891	
892	    .. versionchanged:: 2.3
893	       Basic support for translation comments was added.  If `comment_tags`
894	       is now set to a list of keywords for extraction, the extractor will
895	       try to find the best preceding comment that begins with one of the
896	       keywords.  For best results, make sure to not have more than one
897	       gettext call in one line of code and the matching comment in the
898	       same line or the line before.
899	
900	    .. versionchanged:: 2.5.1
901	       The `newstyle_gettext` flag can be set to `True` to enable newstyle
902	       gettext calls.
903	
904	    .. versionchanged:: 2.7
905	       A `silent` option can now be provided.  If set to `False` template
906	       syntax errors are propagated instead of being ignored.
907	
908	    :param fileobj: the file-like object the messages should be extracted from
909	    :param keywords: a list of keywords (i.e. function names) that should be
910	                     recognized as translation functions
911	    :param comment_tags: a list of translator tags to search for and include
912	                         in the results.
913	    :param options: a dictionary of additional options (optional)
914	    :return: an iterator over ``(lineno, funcname, message, comments)`` tuples.
915	             (comments will be empty currently)
916	    """
917	    extensions: t.Dict[t.Type[Extension], None] = {}
918	
919	    for extension_name in options.get("extensions", "").split(","):
920	        extension_name = extension_name.strip()
921	
922	        if not extension_name:
923	            continue
924	
925	        extensions[import_string(extension_name)] = None
926	
927	    if InternationalizationExtension not in extensions:
928	        extensions[InternationalizationExtension] = None
929	
930	    def getbool(options: t.Mapping[str, str], key: str, default: bool = False) -> bool:
931	        return options.get(key, str(default)).lower() in {"1", "on", "yes", "true"}
932	
933	    silent = getbool(options, "silent", True)
934	    environment = Environment(
935	        options.get("block_start_string", defaults.BLOCK_START_STRING),
936	        options.get("block_end_string", defaults.BLOCK_END_STRING),
937	        options.get("variable_start_string", defaults.VARIABLE_START_STRING),
938	        options.get("variable_end_string", defaults.VARIABLE_END_STRING),
939	        options.get("comment_start_string", defaults.COMMENT_START_STRING),
940	        options.get("comment_end_string", defaults.COMMENT_END_STRING),
941	        options.get("line_statement_prefix") or defaults.LINE_STATEMENT_PREFIX,
942	        options.get("line_comment_prefix") or defaults.LINE_COMMENT_PREFIX,
943	        getbool(options, "trim_blocks", defaults.TRIM_BLOCKS),
944	        getbool(options, "lstrip_blocks", defaults.LSTRIP_BLOCKS),
945	        defaults.NEWLINE_SEQUENCE,
946	        getbool(options, "keep_trailing_newline", defaults.KEEP_TRAILING_NEWLINE),
947	        tuple(extensions),
948	        cache_size=0,
949	        auto_reload=False,
950	    )
951	
952	    if getbool(options, "trimmed"):
953	        environment.policies["ext.i18n.trimmed"] = True
954	    if getbool(options, "newstyle_gettext"):
955	        environment.newstyle_gettext = True  # type: ignore
956	
957	    source = fileobj.read().decode(options.get("encoding", "utf-8"))
958	    try:
959	        node = environment.parse(source)
960	        tokens = list(environment.lex(environment.preprocess(source)))
961	    except TemplateSyntaxError:
962	        if not silent:
963	            raise
964	        # skip templates with syntax errors
965	        return
966	
967	    finder = _CommentFinder(tokens, comment_tags)
968	    for lineno, func, message in extract_from_ast(node, keywords):
969	        yield lineno, func, message, finder.find_comments(lineno)
970	
971	
972	#: nicer import names
973	i18n = InternationalizationExtension
974	do = ExprStmtExtension
975	loopcontrols = LoopControlExtension
976	debug = DebugExtension
977

"""Extension API for adding custom tags and behavior."""
import pprint
import re
import typing as t

from markupsafe import Markup

from . import defaults
from . import nodes
from .environment import Environment
from .exceptions import TemplateAssertionError
from .exceptions import TemplateSyntaxError
from .runtime import concat  # type: ignore
from .runtime import Context
from .runtime import Undefined
from .utils import import_string
from .utils import pass_context

if t.TYPE_CHECKING:
    import typing_extensions as te
    from .lexer import Token
    from .lexer import TokenStream
    from .parser import Parser

    class _TranslationsBasic(te.Protocol):
        def gettext(self, message: str) -> str:
            ...

        def ngettext(self, singular: str, plural: str, n: int) -> str:
            pass

    class _TranslationsContext(_TranslationsBasic):
        def pgettext(self, context: str, message: str) -> str:
            ...

        def npgettext(self, context: str, singular: str, plural: str, n: int) -> str:
            ...

    _SupportedTranslations = t.Union[_TranslationsBasic, _TranslationsContext]


# I18N functions available in Jinja templates. If the I18N library
# provides ugettext, it will be assigned to gettext.
GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS: t.Tuple[str, ...] = (
    "_",
    "gettext",
    "ngettext",
    "pgettext",
    "npgettext",
)
_ws_re = re.compile(r"\s*\n\s*")


class Extension:
    """Extensions can be used to add extra functionality to the Jinja template
    system at the parser level.  Custom extensions are bound to an environment
    but may not store environment specific data on `self`.  The reason for
    this is that an extension can be bound to another environment (for
    overlays) by creating a copy and reassigning the `environment` attribute.

    As extensions are created by the environment they cannot accept any
    arguments for configuration.  One may want to work around that by using
    a factory function, but that is not possible as extensions are identified
    by their import name.  The correct way to configure the extension is
    storing the configuration values on the environment.  Because this way the
    environment ends up acting as central configuration storage the
    attributes may clash which is why extensions have to ensure that the names
    they choose for configuration are not too generic.  ``prefix`` for example
    is a terrible name, ``fragment_cache_prefix`` on the other hand is a good
    name as includes the name of the extension (fragment cache).
    """

    identifier: t.ClassVar[str]

    def __init_subclass__(cls) -> None:
        cls.identifier = f"{cls.__module__}.{cls.__name__}"

    #: if this extension parses this is the list of tags it's listening to.
    tags: t.Set[str] = set()

    #: the priority of that extension.  This is especially useful for
    #: extensions that preprocess values.  A lower value means higher
    #: priority.
    #:
    #: .. versionadded:: 2.4
    priority = 100

    def __init__(self, environment: Environment) -> None:
        self.environment = environment

    def bind(self, environment: Environment) -> "Extension":
        """Create a copy of this extension bound to another environment."""
        rv = object.__new__(self.__class__)
        rv.__dict__.update(self.__dict__)
        rv.environment = environment
        return rv

    def preprocess(
        self, source: str, name: t.Optional[str], filename: t.Optional[str] = None
    ) -> str:
        """This method is called before the actual lexing and can be used to
        preprocess the source.  The `filename` is optional.  The return value
        must be the preprocessed source.
        """
        return source

    def filter_stream(
        self, stream: "TokenStream"
    ) -> t.Union["TokenStream", t.Iterable["Token"]]:
        """It's passed a :class:`~jinja2.lexer.TokenStream` that can be used
        to filter tokens returned.  This method has to return an iterable of
        :class:`~jinja2.lexer.Token`\\s, but it doesn't have to return a
        :class:`~jinja2.lexer.TokenStream`.
        """
        return stream

    def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """If any of the :attr:`tags` matched this method is called with the
        parser as first argument.  The token the parser stream is pointing at
        is the name token that matched.  This method has to return one or a
        list of multiple nodes.
        """
        raise NotImplementedError()

    def attr(
        self, name: str, lineno: t.Optional[int] = None
    ) -> nodes.ExtensionAttribute:
        """Return an attribute node for the current extension.  This is useful
        to pass constants on extensions to generated template code.

        ::

            self.attr('_my_attribute', lineno=lineno)
        """
        return nodes.ExtensionAttribute(self.identifier, name, lineno=lineno)

    def call_method(
        self,
        name: str,
        args: t.Optional[t.List[nodes.Expr]] = None,
        kwargs: t.Optional[t.List[nodes.Keyword]] = None,
        dyn_args: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None,
        dyn_kwargs: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None,
        lineno: t.Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Call:
        """Call a method of the extension.  This is a shortcut for
        :meth:`attr` + :class:`jinja2.nodes.Call`.
        """
        if args is None:
            args = []
        if kwargs is None:
            kwargs = []
        return nodes.Call(
            self.attr(name, lineno=lineno),
            args,
            kwargs,
            dyn_args,
            dyn_kwargs,
            lineno=lineno,
        )


@pass_context
def _gettext_alias(
    __context: Context, *args: t.Any, **kwargs: t.Any
) -> t.Union[t.Any, Undefined]:
    return __context.call(__context.resolve("gettext"), *args, **kwargs)


def _make_new_gettext(func: t.Callable[[str], str]) -> t.Callable[..., str]:
    @pass_context
    def gettext(__context: Context, __string: str, **variables: t.Any) -> str:
        rv = __context.call(func, __string)
        if __context.eval_ctx.autoescape:
            rv = Markup(rv)
        # Always treat as a format string, even if there are no
        # variables. This makes translation strings more consistent
        # and predictable. This requires escaping
        return rv % variables  # type: ignore

    return gettext


def _make_new_ngettext(func: t.Callable[[str, str, int], str]) -> t.Callable[..., str]:
    @pass_context
    def ngettext(
        __context: Context,
        __singular: str,
        __plural: str,
        __num: int,
        **variables: t.Any,
    ) -> str:
        variables.setdefault("num", __num)
        rv = __context.call(func, __singular, __plural, __num)
        if __context.eval_ctx.autoescape:
            rv = Markup(rv)
        # Always treat as a format string, see gettext comment above.
        return rv % variables  # type: ignore

    return ngettext


def _make_new_dgettext(func: t.Callable[[str, str], str]) -> t.Callable[..., str]:
    @pass_context
    def dgettext(
        __context: Context, __domain: str, __string: str, **variables: t.Any
    ) -> str:
        rv = __context.call(func, __domain, __string)
        if __context.eval_ctx.autoescape:
            rv = Markup(rv)
        # Always treat as a format string, even if there are no
        # variables. This makes translation strings more consistent
        # and predictable. This requires escaping
        return rv % variables  # type: ignore

    return dgettext


def _make_new_dngettext(
    func: t.Callable[[str, str, str, int], str]
) -> t.Callable[..., str]:
    @pass_context
    def dngettext(
        __context: Context,
        __domain: str,
        __singular: str,
        __plural: str,
        __num: int,
        **variables: t.Any,
    ) -> str:
        variables.setdefault("num", __num)
        rv = __context.call(func, __domain, __singular, __plural, __num)
        if __context.eval_ctx.autoescape:
            rv = Markup(rv)
        # Always treat as a format string, see gettext comment above.
        return rv % variables  # type: ignore

    return dngettext


def _make_new_pgettext(func: t.Callable[[str, str], str]) -> t.Callable[..., str]:
    @pass_context
    def pgettext(
        __context: Context, __string_ctx: str, __string: str, **variables: t.Any
    ) -> str:
        variables.setdefault("context", __string_ctx)
        rv = __context.call(func, __string_ctx, __string)

        if __context.eval_ctx.autoescape:
            rv = Markup(rv)

        # Always treat as a format string, see gettext comment above.
        return rv % variables  # type: ignore

    return pgettext


def _make_new_npgettext(
    func: t.Callable[[str, str, str, int], str]
) -> t.Callable[..., str]:
    @pass_context
    def npgettext(
        __context: Context,
        __string_ctx: str,
        __singular: str,
        __plural: str,
        __num: int,
        **variables: t.Any,
    ) -> str:
        variables.setdefault("context", __string_ctx)
        variables.setdefault("num", __num)
        rv = __context.call(func, __string_ctx, __singular, __plural, __num)

        if __context.eval_ctx.autoescape:
            rv = Markup(rv)

        # Always treat as a format string, see gettext comment above.
        return rv % variables  # type: ignore

    return npgettext


class InternationalizationExtension(Extension):
    """This extension adds gettext support to Jinja."""

    tags = {"trans"}

    # TODO: the i18n extension is currently reevaluating values in a few
    # situations.  Take this example:
    #   {% trans count=something() %}{{ count }} foo{% pluralize
    #     %}{{ count }} fooss{% endtrans %}
    # something is called twice here.  One time for the gettext value and
    # the other time for the n-parameter of the ngettext function.

    def __init__(self, environment: Environment) -> None:
        super().__init__(environment)
        environment.globals["_"] = _gettext_alias
        environment.extend(
            install_gettext_translations=self._install,
            install_null_translations=self._install_null,
            install_gettext_callables=self._install_callables,
            uninstall_gettext_translations=self._uninstall,
            extract_translations=self._extract,
            newstyle_gettext=False,
        )

    def _install(
        self, translations: "_SupportedTranslations", newstyle: t.Optional[bool] = None
    ) -> None:
        # ugettext and ungettext are preferred in case the I18N library
        # is providing compatibility with older Python versions.
        gettext = getattr(translations, "ugettext", None)
        if gettext is None:
            gettext = translations.gettext
        ngettext = getattr(translations, "ungettext", None)
        if ngettext is None:
            ngettext = translations.ngettext

        pgettext = getattr(translations, "pgettext", None)
        npgettext = getattr(translations, "npgettext", None)
        dgettext = getattr(translations, "dgettext", None)
        dngettext = getattr(translations, "dngettext", None)
        self._install_callables(
            gettext,
            ngettext,
            newstyle=newstyle,
            pgettext=pgettext,
            npgettext=npgettext,
            dgettext=dgettext,
            dngettext=dngettext,
        )

    def _install_null(self, newstyle: t.Optional[bool] = None) -> None:
        import gettext

        translations = gettext.NullTranslations()

        if hasattr(translations, "pgettext"):
            # Python < 3.8
            pgettext = translations.pgettext  # type: ignore
        else:

            def pgettext(c: str, s: str) -> str:
                return s

        if hasattr(translations, "npgettext"):
            npgettext = translations.npgettext  # type: ignore
        else:

            def npgettext(c: str, s: str, p: str, n: int) -> str:
                return s if n == 1 else p

        if hasattr(translations, "dgettext"):
            dgettext = translations.dgettext  # type: ignore
        else:

            def dgettext(d: str, s: str) -> str:
                return translations.gettext(s)

        if hasattr(translations, "dngettext"):
            dngettext = translations.dngettext  # type: ignore
        else:

            def dngettext(d: str, s: str, p: str, n: int) -> str:
                return translations.ngettext(s, p, n)

        self._install_callables(
            gettext=translations.gettext,
            ngettext=translations.ngettext,
            newstyle=newstyle,
            pgettext=pgettext,
            npgettext=npgettext,
            dgettext=dgettext,
            dngettext=dngettext,
        )

    def _install_callables(
        self,
        gettext: t.Callable[[str], str],
        ngettext: t.Callable[[str, str, int], str],
        newstyle: t.Optional[bool] = None,
        pgettext: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str, str], str]] = None,
        npgettext: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str, str, str, int], str]] = None,
        dgettext: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str, str], str]] = None,
        dngettext: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str, str, str, int], str]] = None,
    ) -> None:
        if newstyle is not None:
            self.environment.newstyle_gettext = newstyle  # type: ignore
        if self.environment.newstyle_gettext:  # type: ignore
            gettext = _make_new_gettext(gettext)
            ngettext = _make_new_ngettext(ngettext)

            if pgettext is not None:
                pgettext = _make_new_pgettext(pgettext)

            if npgettext is not None:
                npgettext = _make_new_npgettext(npgettext)

            if dgettext is not None:
                dgettext = _make_new_dgettext(dgettext)

            if dngettext is not None:
                dngettext = _make_new_dngettext(dngettext)

        self.environment.globals.update(
            gettext=gettext,
            ngettext=ngettext,
            pgettext=pgettext,
            npgettext=npgettext,
            dgettext=dgettext,
            dngettext=dngettext,
        )

    def _uninstall(self, translations: "_SupportedTranslations") -> None:
        for key in (
            "gettext",
            "ngettext",
            "pgettext",
            "npgettext",
            "dgettext",
            "dngettext",
        ):
            self.environment.globals.pop(key, None)

    def _extract(
        self,
        source: t.Union[str, nodes.Template],
        gettext_functions: t.Sequence[str] = GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS,
    ) -> t.Iterator[
        t.Tuple[int, str, t.Union[t.Optional[str], t.Tuple[t.Optional[str], ...]]]
    ]:
        if isinstance(source, str):
            source = self.environment.parse(source)
        return extract_from_ast(source, gettext_functions)

    def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """Parse a translatable tag."""
        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
        num_called_num = False

        # find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
        # defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at
        # a later state.
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
        trimmed = None
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None
        while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
            if variables:
                parser.stream.expect("comma")

            # skip colon for python compatibility
            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
                break

            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
            if token.value in variables:
                parser.fail(
                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            # expressions
            if token.value == "domain" and parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)
                if domain is not None:
                    parser.fail(
                        "translation domain given twice.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                domain_lineno = parser.stream.current.lineno
                domain_expr = parser.parse_expression()
                if not isinstance(domain_expr, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(
                    domain_expr.value, str
                ):
                    parser.fail(
                        "translation domain must be a string literal (constant),"
                        " not a variable.",
                        domain_lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                domain = domain_expr.value
                # allow a following comma even though "domain" isn't
                # tracked in `variables`, so it can be freely mixed in
                # with the other comma-separated parameters
                parser.stream.skip_if("comma")
                continue
            elif parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)
                variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
                continue
            else:
                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

            if plural_expr is None:
                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
                        nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var
                    )
                else:
                    plural_expr = var
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"

        parser.stream.expect("block_end")

        plural = None
        have_plural = False
        referenced = set()

        # now parse until endtrans or pluralize
        singular_names, singular = self._parse_block(parser, True)
        if singular_names:
            referenced.update(singular_names)
            if plural_expr is None:
                plural_expr = nodes.Name(singular_names[0], "load")
                num_called_num = singular_names[0] == "num"

        # if we have a pluralize block, we parse that too
        if parser.stream.current.test("name:pluralize"):
            have_plural = True
            next(parser.stream)
            if parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
                token = parser.stream.expect("name")
                if token.value not in variables:
                    parser.fail(
                        f"unknown variable {token.value!r} for pluralization",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                plural_expr = variables[token.value]
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"
            parser.stream.expect("block_end")
            plural_names, plural = self._parse_block(parser, False)
            next(parser.stream)
            referenced.update(plural_names)
        else:
            next(parser.stream)

        # register free names as simple name expressions
        for name in referenced:
            if name not in variables:
                variables[name] = nodes.Name(name, "load")

        if not have_plural:
            plural_expr = None
        elif plural_expr is None:
            parser.fail("pluralize without variables", lineno)

        if trimmed is None:
            trimmed = self.environment.policies["ext.i18n.trimmed"]
        if trimmed:
            singular = self._trim_whitespace(singular)
            if plural:
                plural = self._trim_whitespace(plural)

        node = self._make_node(
            singular,
            plural,
            variables,
            plural_expr,
            bool(referenced),
            num_called_num and have_plural,
            domain,
        )
        node.set_lineno(lineno)
        if plural_expr_assignment is not None:
            return [plural_expr_assignment, node]
        else:
            return node

    def _trim_whitespace(self, string: str, _ws_re: t.Pattern[str] = _ws_re) -> str:
        return _ws_re.sub(" ", string.strip())

    def _parse_block(
        self, parser: "Parser", allow_pluralize: bool
    ) -> t.Tuple[t.List[str], str]:
        """Parse until the next block tag with a given name."""
        referenced = []
        buf = []

        while True:
            if parser.stream.current.type == "data":
                buf.append(parser.stream.current.value.replace("%", "%%"))
                next(parser.stream)
            elif parser.stream.current.type == "variable_begin":
                next(parser.stream)
                name = parser.stream.expect("name").value
                referenced.append(name)
                buf.append(f"%({name})s")
                parser.stream.expect("variable_end")
            elif parser.stream.current.type == "block_begin":
                next(parser.stream)
                if parser.stream.current.test("name:endtrans"):
                    break
                elif parser.stream.current.test("name:pluralize"):
                    if allow_pluralize:
                        break
                    parser.fail(
                        "a translatable section can have only one pluralize section"
                    )
                parser.fail(
                    "control structures in translatable sections are not allowed"
                )
            elif parser.stream.eos:
                parser.fail("unclosed translation block")
            else:
                raise RuntimeError("internal parser error")

        return referenced, concat(buf)

    def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            if domain is not None:
                func_name = "dgettext"
                func_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = [
                    nodes.Const(domain),
                    nodes.Const(singular),
                ]
            else:
                func_name = "gettext"
                func_args = [nodes.Const(singular)]
            node = nodes.Call(nodes.Name(func_name, "load"), func_args, [], None, None)

        # singular and plural
        else:
            if domain is not None:
                func_name = "dngettext"
                func_args = [
                    nodes.Const(domain),
                    nodes.Const(singular),
                    nodes.Const(plural),
                    plural_expr,
                ]
            else:
                func_name = "ngettext"
                func_args = [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr]
            node = nodes.Call(nodes.Name(func_name, "load"), func_args, [], None, None)

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )
        return nodes.Output([node])


class ExprStmtExtension(Extension):
    """Adds a `do` tag to Jinja that works like the print statement just
    that it doesn't print the return value.
    """

    tags = {"do"}

    def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> nodes.ExprStmt:
        node = nodes.ExprStmt(lineno=next(parser.stream).lineno)
        node.node = parser.parse_tuple()
        return node


class LoopControlExtension(Extension):
    """Adds break and continue to the template engine."""

    tags = {"break", "continue"}

    def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Break, nodes.Continue]:
        token = next(parser.stream)
        if token.value == "break":
            return nodes.Break(lineno=token.lineno)
        return nodes.Continue(lineno=token.lineno)


class DebugExtension(Extension):
    """A ``{% debug %}`` tag that dumps the available variables,
    filters, and tests.

    .. code-block:: html+jinja

        <pre>{% debug %}</pre>

    .. code-block:: text

        {'context': {'cycler': <class 'jinja2.utils.Cycler'>,
                     ...,
                     'namespace': <class 'jinja2.utils.Namespace'>},
         'filters': ['abs', 'attr', 'batch', 'capitalize', 'center', 'count', 'd',
                     ..., 'urlencode', 'urlize', 'wordcount', 'wordwrap', 'xmlattr'],
         'tests': ['!=', '<', '<=', '==', '>', '>=', 'callable', 'defined',
                   ..., 'odd', 'sameas', 'sequence', 'string', 'undefined', 'upper']}

    .. versionadded:: 2.11.0
    """

    tags = {"debug"}

    def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> nodes.Output:
        lineno = parser.stream.expect("name:debug").lineno
        context = nodes.ContextReference()
        result = self.call_method("_render", [context], lineno=lineno)
        return nodes.Output([result], lineno=lineno)

    def _render(self, context: Context) -> str:
        result = {
            "context": context.get_all(),
            "filters": sorted(self.environment.filters.keys()),
            "tests": sorted(self.environment.tests.keys()),
        }

        # Set the depth since the intent is to show the top few names.
        return pprint.pformat(result, depth=3, compact=True)


def extract_from_ast(
    ast: nodes.Template,
    gettext_functions: t.Sequence[str] = GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS,
    babel_style: bool = True,
) -> t.Iterator[
    t.Tuple[int, str, t.Union[t.Optional[str], t.Tuple[t.Optional[str], ...]]]
]:
    """Extract localizable strings from the given template node.  Per
    default this function returns matches in babel style that means non string
    parameters as well as keyword arguments are returned as `None`.  This
    allows Babel to figure out what you really meant if you are using
    gettext functions that allow keyword arguments for placeholder expansion.
    If you don't want that behavior set the `babel_style` parameter to `False`
    which causes only strings to be returned and parameters are always stored
    in tuples.  As a consequence invalid gettext calls (calls without a single
    string parameter or string parameters after non-string parameters) are
    skipped.

    This example explains the behavior:

    >>> from jinja2 import Environment
    >>> env = Environment()
    >>> node = env.parse('{{ (_("foo"), _(), ngettext("foo", "bar", 42)) }}')
    >>> list(extract_from_ast(node))
    [(1, '_', 'foo'), (1, '_', ()), (1, 'ngettext', ('foo', 'bar', None))]
    >>> list(extract_from_ast(node, babel_style=False))
    [(1, '_', ('foo',)), (1, 'ngettext', ('foo', 'bar'))]

    For every string found this function yields a ``(lineno, function,
    message)`` tuple, where:

    * ``lineno`` is the number of the line on which the string was found,
    * ``function`` is the name of the ``gettext`` function used (if the
      string was extracted from embedded Python code), and
    *   ``message`` is the string, or a tuple of strings for functions
         with multiple string arguments.

    This extraction function operates on the AST and is because of that unable
    to extract any comments.  For comment support you have to use the babel
    extraction interface or extract comments yourself.
    """
    out: t.Union[t.Optional[str], t.Tuple[t.Optional[str], ...]]

    for node in ast.find_all(nodes.Call):
        if (
            not isinstance(node.node, nodes.Name)
            or node.node.name not in gettext_functions
        ):
            continue

        strings: t.List[t.Optional[str]] = []

        for arg in node.args:
            if isinstance(arg, nodes.Const) and isinstance(arg.value, str):
                strings.append(arg.value)
            else:
                strings.append(None)

        for _ in node.kwargs:
            strings.append(None)
        if node.dyn_args is not None:
            strings.append(None)
        if node.dyn_kwargs is not None:
            strings.append(None)

        if not babel_style:
            out = tuple(x for x in strings if x is not None)

            if not out:
                continue
        else:
            if len(strings) == 1:
                out = strings[0]
            else:
                out = tuple(strings)

        yield node.lineno, node.node.name, out


class _CommentFinder:
    """Helper class to find comments in a token stream.  Can only
    find comments for gettext calls forwards.  Once the comment
    from line 4 is found, a comment for line 1 will not return a
    usable value.
    """

    def __init__(
        self, tokens: t.Sequence[t.Tuple[int, str, str]], comment_tags: t.Sequence[str]
    ) -> None:
        self.tokens = tokens
        self.comment_tags = comment_tags
        self.offset = 0
        self.last_lineno = 0

    def find_backwards(self, offset: int) -> t.List[str]:
        try:
            for _, token_type, token_value in reversed(
                self.tokens[self.offset : offset]
            ):
                if token_type in ("comment", "linecomment"):
                    try:
                        prefix, comment = token_value.split(None, 1)
                    except ValueError:
                        continue
                    if prefix in self.comment_tags:
                        return [comment.rstrip()]
            return []
        finally:
            self.offset = offset

    def find_comments(self, lineno: int) -> t.List[str]:
        if not self.comment_tags or self.last_lineno > lineno:
            return []
        for idx, (token_lineno, _, _) in enumerate(self.tokens[self.offset :]):
            if token_lineno > lineno:
                return self.find_backwards(self.offset + idx)
        return self.find_backwards(len(self.tokens))


def babel_extract(
    fileobj: t.BinaryIO,
    keywords: t.Sequence[str],
    comment_tags: t.Sequence[str],
    options: t.Dict[str, t.Any],
) -> t.Iterator[
    t.Tuple[
        int, str, t.Union[t.Optional[str], t.Tuple[t.Optional[str], ...]], t.List[str]
    ]
]:
    """Babel extraction method for Jinja templates.

    .. versionchanged:: 2.3
       Basic support for translation comments was added.  If `comment_tags`
       is now set to a list of keywords for extraction, the extractor will
       try to find the best preceding comment that begins with one of the
       keywords.  For best results, make sure to not have more than one
       gettext call in one line of code and the matching comment in the
       same line or the line before.

    .. versionchanged:: 2.5.1
       The `newstyle_gettext` flag can be set to `True` to enable newstyle
       gettext calls.

    .. versionchanged:: 2.7
       A `silent` option can now be provided.  If set to `False` template
       syntax errors are propagated instead of being ignored.

    :param fileobj: the file-like object the messages should be extracted from
    :param keywords: a list of keywords (i.e. function names) that should be
                     recognized as translation functions
    :param comment_tags: a list of translator tags to search for and include
                         in the results.
    :param options: a dictionary of additional options (optional)
    :return: an iterator over ``(lineno, funcname, message, comments)`` tuples.
             (comments will be empty currently)
    """
    extensions: t.Dict[t.Type[Extension], None] = {}

    for extension_name in options.get("extensions", "").split(","):
        extension_name = extension_name.strip()

        if not extension_name:
            continue

        extensions[import_string(extension_name)] = None

    if InternationalizationExtension not in extensions:
        extensions[InternationalizationExtension] = None

    def getbool(options: t.Mapping[str, str], key: str, default: bool = False) -> bool:
        return options.get(key, str(default)).lower() in {"1", "on", "yes", "true"}

    silent = getbool(options, "silent", True)
    environment = Environment(
        options.get("block_start_string", defaults.BLOCK_START_STRING),
        options.get("block_end_string", defaults.BLOCK_END_STRING),
        options.get("variable_start_string", defaults.VARIABLE_START_STRING),
        options.get("variable_end_string", defaults.VARIABLE_END_STRING),
        options.get("comment_start_string", defaults.COMMENT_START_STRING),
        options.get("comment_end_string", defaults.COMMENT_END_STRING),
        options.get("line_statement_prefix") or defaults.LINE_STATEMENT_PREFIX,
        options.get("line_comment_prefix") or defaults.LINE_COMMENT_PREFIX,
        getbool(options, "trim_blocks", defaults.TRIM_BLOCKS),
        getbool(options, "lstrip_blocks", defaults.LSTRIP_BLOCKS),
        defaults.NEWLINE_SEQUENCE,
        getbool(options, "keep_trailing_newline", defaults.KEEP_TRAILING_NEWLINE),
        tuple(extensions),
        cache_size=0,
        auto_reload=False,
    )

    if getbool(options, "trimmed"):
        environment.policies["ext.i18n.trimmed"] = True
    if getbool(options, "newstyle_gettext"):
        environment.newstyle_gettext = True  # type: ignore

    source = fileobj.read().decode(options.get("encoding", "utf-8"))
    try:
        node = environment.parse(source)
        tokens = list(environment.lex(environment.preprocess(source)))
    except TemplateSyntaxError:
        if not silent:
            raise
        # skip templates with syntax errors
        return

    finder = _CommentFinder(tokens, comment_tags)
    for lineno, func, message in extract_from_ast(node, keywords):
        yield lineno, func, message, finder.find_comments(lineno)


#: nicer import names
i18n = InternationalizationExtension
do = ExprStmtExtension
loopcontrols = LoopControlExtension
debug = DebugExtension

+    @pass_context

All 830 existing tests pass; patch.txt written."

content.text:
## Summary

Implemented domain parameter support for the `{% trans %}` tag in `src/jinja2/ext.py`:

- **`parse()`**: added a `domain` special-case in the parameter loop that requires a string-literal value, raises `TemplateAssertionError` for duplicate domains ("translation domain given twice") or non-literal values ("translation domain must be a string literal (constant), not a variable"), and allows a comma to follow the domain clause so it can be freely combined with other parameters.
- **`_make_node()`**: now takes an optional `domain` argument and emits `dgettext(domain, message)` / `dngettext(domain, singular, plural, n)` calls instead of `gettext`/`ngettext` when a domain is given.
- **`_install_callables()` / `_install()` / `_install_null()` / `_uninstall()`**: extended to plumb `dgettext`/`dngettext` through to environment globals, including newstyle wrapping (new `_make_new_dgettext`/`_make_new_dngettext` helpers) with variable substitution and autoescape handling matching the existing `gettext`/`ngettext` pattern.

Verified manually with old-style and newstyle gettext, singular/plural, `install_null_translations`, and all error paths; the full existing suite (830 tests) still passes. Only `src/jinja2/ext.py` was touched; diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.

result:
## Summary

Implemented domain parameter support for the `{% trans %}` tag in `src/jinja2/ext.py`:

- **`parse()`**: added a `domain` special-case in the parameter loop that requires a string-literal value, raises `TemplateAssertionError` for duplicate domains ("translation domain given twice") or non-literal values ("translation domain must be a string literal (constant), not a variable"), and allows a comma to follow the domain clause so it can be freely combined with other parameters.
- **`_make_node()`**: now takes an optional `domain` argument and emits `dgettext(domain, message)` / `dngettext(domain, singular, plural, n)` calls instead of `gettext`/`ngettext` when a domain is given.
- **`_install_callables()` / `_install()` / `_install_null()` / `_uninstall()`**: extended to plumb `dgettext`/`dngettext` through to environment globals, including newstyle wrapping (new `_make_new_dgettext`/`_make_new_dngettext` helpers) with variable substitution and autoescape handling matching the existing `gettext`/`ngettext` pattern.

Verified manually with old-style and newstyle gettext, singular/plural, `install_null_translations`, and all error paths; the full existing suite (830 tests) still passes. Only `src/jinja2/ext.py` was touched; diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.
